concerns.text: SC-001 defines success as 'Moran's I p-value > 0.05' (insignificant autocorrelation) OR 'reduction ≥ 10%'. The first condition (p > 0.05) is statistically unsound for model validation in this context. Failing to reject the null hypothesis (that residuals are random) is not evidence of the alternative (that the model is correct); it may simply indicate low statistical power due to sample size or weak signal. A model can be misspecified yet still yield p > 0.05. Success should be defined by the *magnitude* of reduction in the Moran's I statistic (effect size) or information criteria (AIC), not by a binary hypothesis test failure which is a non-falsifiable 'success' condition for a flawed model.

SC-002 requires a 'statistically significant reduction in RMSE... via Diebold-Mariano test'. The Diebold-Mariano (DM) test is designed for comparing *time series* forecast accuracy (testing if forecast errors are uncorrelated over time). Applying DM to *spatial* cross-validation folds is a category error. Spatial dependence violates the independence assumption of standard DM tests. The spec must specify a spatially-aware comparison test (e.g., a permutation test on spatial blocks or a spatial block bootstrap) to validly claim statistical significance of the RMSE difference.

FR-009 mandates Benjamini-Hochberg (BH) FDR correction for covariate p-values. In spatial regression (Lag/Error models), the effective degrees of freedom are reduced due to spatial autocorrelation, and the standard errors of coefficients are often biased if the spatial structure is misspecified. Applying standard BH correction (which assumes independent or specific dependency structures) to spatially correlated coefficient estimates without adjusting for the effective sample size or using spatially robust standard errors may yield invalid FDR control. The methodology needs to specify the use of spatially robust inference (e.g., Conley standard errors or cluster-robust SEs) before applying FDR.

The assumption 'if a specific variable is missing, a [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] will be raised' contains a literal placeholder marker '[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]'. Per the kickback instructions, a converged spec must not carry this marker. The text must be revised to state the fallback procedure explicitly (e.g., '...a proxy variable will be selected from [List] or the analysis will be restricted to available variables') rather than deferring the decision to a future undefined state.
